Narrative_Meaning: Jacks signify youth, high spirits, parties, quarrels, protection, privilege and disagreements.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Explorer card represents the urge to wander, to seek out new places, and make new discoveries. Exploration, investigation and travel are all indicated by the card, but there is another side to the Explorer, sometimes they come as an invader, or a crusader. If you are the Explorer it warns you that you may not be seen as just a tourist by the natives.

Significator.Plot: The Explorer Plot is another good world-builder (although the Yarn-Teller does have to a little more work. The Explorer plot is about exploring a blank map. The map may be of a new land or new world, but the Characters are Hooked to explore and investigate the map, of course each area they go may consider them invaders.
Significator.Side: A side marked by the Explorer behaves as though they are an explorer in a strange land. This may be because they are, or they may be experiencing life beyond their class for the first time, the naive country bumpkin who wishes to be a king's guard does not understand things in the city, the princess on the run does not understand why anyone does anything they do, and why none of them do what she tells them. Such Characters are rarely out of trouble for long...
Significator.Character:
The Explorer Significator indicates a Character that lives to explore and investigate.

They Gain Yin when they are travel more than 1 kilometre.

They Gain Yang when they explore or investigate a place or person.

They Gain Chi when they make notes, drawings or maps of a location, person or thing.

They also gain +1 Success Levels when they are in unfamiliar territory.
Significator.Scene: The Explorer Scene indicates a Scene where the Character has to explore a Location, or meets an explorer or invader. Such Scenes are often Ordeals although a few may be Wefts. Yarn-Tellers may play the card to "create" a new Location to be explored, or make a Location seem unfamiliar (possibly by side-stepping) or summon an explorer ally or invading enemy for the target.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with an unexpected visitor that may  bring the PCs a Revelation and a Gain. This Visitor is usually a friend or Ally, rather than a Rival or enemy, but occasionally it will be a stranger, who claims a connection that will later be validated or proved a lie.
Yarn.Gain: Discover a new or lost Location / Real Estate / Province / Dark Territory
Yarn.Fray_Description: The Explore Fray is an exploration of the Conflict, it is time to explore some aspect of the interactions of the Facets in the Conflict. This may be done by actually exploring a Locations that Embodies the Conflict directly, or investigating a Descendant that has an Annex like the Conflict's.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The PCs actions are complicated by there being no map, no GPS or the streets just seem different. The PCs or those they seek are lost, this can be due to High Strangeness like a Yarn-Teller or Time-Traveller side-stepping or ret-conning the area and making the map change.

NPC.Description: Captains are commanding officers who follow others orders and act as eyes and ears abroad. They are investigators, scouts and spies as much as they are explorers or leaders.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs discover, explore, or gain access to a map or directions, that includes a secret passage, short cut or hidden way. This will allow them to appear or escape via this short cut and avoid any police, guards or enemy forces. It may allow them to skip Stages of the next Fray Ordeal or similar.
Yarn.History_Description: The Explorer indicates that the current situation has roots in an old exploration. The area explored or the explorer may be more important to the current situation.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Explorer indicates that the current situation involves being lost, usually by having a clear idea where you want to be, but no idea which direction that may lie in.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Explorer indicates that there is a solution to the current situation, but it will involve some navigation. Sometimes navigation will have a map, or a compass, other times you'll have to draw the map

Revelations.About_Description: The Information is specifically about the Opposed Embodiment, or a particular Rival of the Yarn-Teller.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about a Character, usually an Embodiment. It might be their name, or genealogy, perhaps their CV.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information comes via a communications technology, perhaps the radio, or a cell phone call, or in another age a messenger or pigeon.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about changes that have occurred to or because of the Embodiment. It can also signify that the information itself changes. Appearing as one thing to begin with, but then being later revealed by a detail to be something else (or could magically change).
Revelations.Detail_Description: Delicate details are the finer points, little bits of information that might be overlooked easily, and could even be destroyed accidentally before they can be properly examined. A delicate detail might indicate a quirk, vulnerability, or flaw in some cases, but will be hard to spot. "He was a rakish, whip of a man, dressed in black leather, mail and silks. His eyes were dark and tired, close set, above a thin nose, broad cheekbones and confident grin..."

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked, but by a maze or labyrinth that represents a complex, difficult to memorise or work out route. A Maze can be handled as an Ordeal within an Ordeal (with finer detail on the Stages, corridors instead of roads, or cubicles instead of rooms), or simply as a Navigation test, fail and you are lost in the maze... Fail again and you may be lost more than that.

Tarot.Normal: Target may ignore Range Difficulties and add +1 Success Levels when acting at Range, all Ranged Attacks increase in Stake 1 Level (to Extreme) while atop pile.
Tarot.Crossed: Target takes -2 Success Levels and Range costs +18 Pips (+36 Score) to all actions, all Ranged Attacks decrease in STake 1 Level (to Low) while atop pile.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 Obstacle in every Non-Court Stage, 2 in every Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: Jump (or balance) over danger. Leaping between catwalks over molten metal, missing slats on rope bridges over unlikely crocodile-filled rapids, or a rooftop run over a burning building, are all good examples.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Swim faster! They're right behind us!
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Ebbing side of the battle is offered a chance to escape, this may be a deliberate tactic by the Flowing side, or just represent an opportunity that appears to them. The Ebbing side will have +1 Pips on any card played in a Disengagement Action, but a -1 Pips on any Attack or Defence Actions.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The Fight is against a powerful soldier or military type, well equipped, with Attack and Defence dedicated Annexes. The Knight is at least equal in power to the most powerful PC, and has Armour and at least two Weapon Descendants. The Knight may also be a Monster.
  Facet    Monster  
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc
Lea.Notes: Broccen may use Jack’s to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.

Stress.Description: Stress can have profound effects on the mind, causing one to see and hear all sorts of things that are not happening. Simply missing too much sleep can cause a healthy mind to micro-sleep and confuse the Character’s real and dream lives, and for some minds the confusion is more easily started, unusual brain chemistry and even normal development can lead to all manner of hallucinatory voices and imaginary friends knocking about the place.
Stress.Rules: The Character’s current Stress and the current Tension Level are added together to create an amount of Chi. This Chi must be paid by the Character  and is used to either purchase a new "Hallucinatory" Character or to side-step the Character partially into a Psycho-social dream space. The Hallucination continues  for a period of time equal to the Current Stress as Intrepid Facet Sway after which the hallucination will vanish removing 1 Stress. Typically the hallucination will reappear anytime the Character’s current Stress plus the Tension Level is high enough to create them again. Hallucinations are typically an extension of the Character, even if they do appear as grey aliens, faeries, ghosts or shadow people, so trying to harm them with magic, for example, will usually harm the Character experiencing the hallucinations, of course sometimes Increated will use hallucinations as the basis of a Nightmare Form, in which case they can be targeted.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can confuse and delude the mind, making a Character who should feel at home, feel like a foreigner, a stranger in a strange land. The Character may suddenly be bothered by their own culture, what was once traditional and reassuring, can become alien and disturbing, or they may feel drawn to explore some other culture, retreating from their own, and the traumas they associate with it.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma will often create a Hitch with Relieved Current Stress, that may reflect this oddness, delusion or strangeness that they feel. The Character will Relieve 1 Stress when act as a stranger or outsider to their own culture in someway, questioning or rejecting aspects of a tradition or cultural more.

Age.Description: An Age of Exploration is occurring. During this age a society explores beyond its borders, seeking unsettled lands that they can add to their own territory. A society often becomes obsessed with the latest exploratory missions, and the discoveries they have found, with new plants, animals, feathers or cultural artifacts. Settlement or colonization of the new land is usually the result of this age.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Exploration a society encounters a new territory that they must explore, uninhabited territories may provide new animals, minerals or plants. Inhabited territories will expose the society to a new external (most often ancient or dead) culture, which captures the public imagination for a while.
